knowledge.description: Standard punctuation mark used to end declarative sentences in many writing systems.
knowledge.history: Derived from ancient Greek punctuation traditions and normalized in modern typography.
knowledge.culturalContext: Sentence-final punctuation conventions differ across languages and scripts.
knowledge.notes: In technical contexts, a period may indicate file extensions, decimal notation, or dot-path syntax.

name: Leftwards Arrow to Bar Over Rightwards Arrow to Bar

knowledge.description: Mathematical operator representing addition and positive quantity.
knowledge.history: The modern plus glyph became widespread in European mathematics during the 15th century.
knowledge.notes: Also reused in medicine, blood typing, and interface labels to indicate increase/addition.

knowledge.description: Currency sign associated with multiple currencies, including USD and several peso-denominated systems.
knowledge.history: Commonly linked to historical abbreviations for peso and real in colonial accounting records.
knowledge.culturalContext: Interpretation depends on locale; ISO currency codes are preferred in multilingual interfaces.

Space
note: Standard word separator.
contextualNote: Standard word separator.

name: No-Break Space
note: Prevents line breaks between words/numbers.
contextualNote: Prevents line breaks between words/numbers.

name: Zero Width Space
note: Invisible separator; can break matching/copy-paste in code and search.
contextualNote: Invisible separator; can break matching/copy-paste in code and search.

name: Zero Width Joiner
note: Joins characters into ligatures/emoji sequences. Invisible but semantically important.
contextualNote: Joins characters into ligatures/emoji sequences. Invisible but semantically important.

name: Zero Width Non-Joiner
note: Prevents joining in scripts with contextual forms.
contextualNote: Prevents joining in scripts with contextual forms.

name: Zero Width No-Break Space / BOM
note: Legacy BOM; may cause parsing issues at file starts.
contextualNote: Legacy BOM; may cause parsing issues at file starts.

knowledge.description: Emoji representing happiness, friendliness, or positive tone in short-form digital text.
knowledge.history: Added in early Unicode emoji sets and standardized with broad cross-platform support.
knowledge.notes: Rendering style varies by platform; perceived emotional intensity can differ by glyph design.

name: Peace Symbol
note: Peace and anti-war symbol used across movements.
contextualNote: Peace and anti-war symbol used across movements.

knowledge.description: Symbol adopted by anti-war and nuclear disarmament movements and later by broader peace activism.
knowledge.history: Designed in 1958 for the British Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament and spread globally in protest culture.
knowledge.culturalContext: May carry different political meanings based on region, era, and movement context.
knowledge.notes: Treat as historically sensitive in educational or moderation-sensitive contexts.

name: Circled Latin Capital Letter A
note: Commonly used as an anarchy symbol in political contexts.
contextualNote: Commonly used as an anarchy symbol in political contexts.

name: Hammer and Sickle
note: Associated with communist movements and state symbolism.
contextualNote: Associated with communist movements and state symbolism.

name: Swastika (Buddhist/Historic Form)
note: Historic religious symbol in Asia; in Western political contexts can be interpreted as extremist imagery.
contextualNote: Historic religious symbol in Asia; in Western political contexts can be interpreted as extremist imagery.

name: Black Flag
note: Used in varied contexts including protest, mourning, and anarchist symbolism.
contextualNote: Used in varied contexts including protest, mourning, and anarchist symbolism.

name: Rainbow Flag
note: Widely used as LGBTQ+ pride symbol and in civil rights contexts.
contextualNote: Widely used as LGBTQ+ pride symbol and in civil rights contexts.

name: Transgender Flag
note: Widely used in transgender rights and identity contexts.
contextualNote: Widely used in transgender rights and identity contexts.

name: Raised Fist
note: General solidarity/protest gesture with context-dependent political meaning.
contextualNote: General solidarity/protest gesture with context-dependent political meaning.

name: Latin Cross
note: Christian symbol used in liturgy and identity.
contextualNote: Christian symbol used in liturgy and identity.

knowledge.description: Cross shape widely used as a Christian religious symbol.
knowledge.history: Cross symbolism evolved from late antiquity into many liturgical, civic, and artistic traditions.
knowledge.culturalContext: Can appear in religious, cultural, memorial, and healthcare-adjacent contexts depending on usage.

name: Star and Crescent
note: Commonly associated with Islam in modern usage.
contextualNote: Commonly associated with Islam in modern usage.

name: Om
note: Sacred symbol in several Dharmic traditions including Hinduism.
contextualNote: Sacred symbol in several Dharmic traditions including Hinduism.

name: Wheel of Dharma
note: Buddhist and Dharmic symbol of teachings/path.
contextualNote: Buddhist and Dharmic symbol of teachings/path.

name: Star of David
note: Jewish religious/cultural symbol.
contextualNote: Jewish religious/cultural symbol.

name: Khanda
note: Primary Sikh emblem.
contextualNote: Primary Sikh emblem.

name: Yin Yang
note: Taoist philosophical and spiritual symbol.
contextualNote: Taoist philosophical and spiritual symbol.

name: Menorah
note: Jewish candelabrum symbol, often used around Hanukkah.
contextualNote: Jewish candelabrum symbol, often used around Hanukkah.

name: Latin Capital Letter N with Tilde

knowledge.description: Runic letter Dagaz (ᛞ), associated with the Elder Futhark script.
knowledge.history: Runic alphabets were used across Germanic-speaking regions from late antiquity into the medieval period.
knowledge.culturalContext: Modern usage appears in linguistics, historical study, and neo-pagan or revival contexts.
